description: High‑level strategy for streamlining the film’s production workflow, integrating virtual production techniques, and optimizing resource allocation to meet budget and schedule targets. Intended for the production, finance, and executive teams.
steps_to_create: Collect baseline production cost and crew data for Hong Kong film projects.
Benchmark current industry practices for virtual and hybrid production workflows.
Define workflow stages, resource requirements, and cost controls.
Present draft to the Producer, Head of Production, and Finance Director for review.
Incorporate stakeholder feedback and finalize the strategy.
approval_authorities: Producer, Head of Production, Finance Director

description: Strategic plan for adapting the original screenplay, defining the level of narrative deviation, and managing the trade‑off between originality and audience familiarity. Target audience: Creative, Production, and Marketing teams.
steps_to_create: Conduct a comparative analysis of the original and proposed scripts.
Facilitate workshops with the Screenwriter, Director, and Lead Actors to explore narrative options.
Assess narrative risks and potential audience reception.
Document the chosen narrative path and contingency options.
Submit the strategy to the Producer and Director for approval.

description: High‑level plan for casting, balancing star power with authenticity, and aligning talent availability with production schedules. Intended for Casting, Production, and Finance stakeholders.
steps_to_create: Compile market data on talent availability, salary ranges, and contractual terms.
Identify candidate actors and directors that fit the creative vision and budget.
Negotiate preliminary agreements and assess scheduling compatibility.
Document the final talent roster and alignment rationale.
Obtain sign‑off from the Producer and Director.

description: Integrated strategy addressing geopolitical risk mitigation and Hong Kong identity amplification, balancing creative freedom with political palatability and cultural authenticity. Targeted at Legal, Creative, and Production leadership.
steps_to_create: Review the Hong Kong Film Censorship Ordinance and recent case law.
Conduct a cultural audit of the script and visual elements.
Identify potential political sensitivities and propose mitigation measures.
Align cultural authenticity with market appeal through stakeholder workshops.
Finalize the strategy and secure approval from the Producer, Director, and Legal Counsel.

description: Comprehensive plan covering marketing, audience engagement tactics, and distribution architecture (theatrical, VOD, streaming). Designed for Marketing, Distribution, and Production leadership.
steps_to_create: Analyze market data on target demographics and distribution channels.
Develop integrated marketing campaigns (trailers, ARG, experiential events).
Define release windows and platform mix (theatrical, premium VOD, streaming).
Align engagement tactics with narrative and cultural strategy.
Present the strategy to the Producer, Marketing Director, and Distribution Manager for approval.

description: Integrated project management plan that includes the risk register, stakeholder communication & engagement plan, funding & budget framework, high‑level schedule, and M&E framework. Serves as the central reference for all project activities.
steps_to_create: Define project scope, objectives, and deliverables.
Create a risk & change management register.
Map stakeholders and develop a communication & engagement plan.
Build a funding & budget framework with cost estimates and funding sources.
Develop an initial high‑level schedule and milestone plan.
Design an M&E framework with KPIs and reporting cadence.
Integrate all components into a single document and circulate for review.
Obtain final approval from the Producer, Executive Producer, and Board.
